meanings.definition: secure with or as if with ropes
examples: tie up the old newspapers and bring them to the recycling shed

meanings.definition: invest so as to make unavailable for other purposes
examples: All my money is tied up in long-term investments

meanings.definition: restrain from moving or operating normally
examples: Traffic is tied up for miles around the bridge where the accident occurred

meanings.definition: secure in or as if in a berth or dock
